features.sectionTitle: Everything you need for your next career move
features.sectionSubtitle: Upload your CV — get a complete picture of where you stand, what's holding you back, and exactly what to do next.

gapAnalysis.title: Know exactly what's holding you back
gapAnalysis.desc: AI identifies your strengths and the specific skill gaps standing between you and your target role. Each gap is rated critical, moderate, or minor — with a closing plan, time estimate, and resources.

salary.title: Find out if you're underpaid
salary.desc: Salary data from government sources (US BLS, UK ONS, EU Eurostat) and developer surveys across 20+ countries. Know your market rate before your next negotiation.

ats.title: Beat the robots that reject 75% of CVs
ats.desc: Paste a job posting to get a keyword match score, missing keywords, and targeted CV fixes. See exactly how your CV stacks up against the ATS filter — before you apply.

coach.title: Ask anything about your next move
coach.desc: Chat with an AI coach that knows your full profile — strengths, gaps, salary data, and career goals. Specific, personalized advice — not generic career tips. (Pro feature)

linkedin.title: Get found by recruiters who have your dream job
linkedin.desc: AI-generated headline options, About section draft, skills to add, and a content strategy — all tailored to make you visible to the right people.

roadmap.title: A concrete plan, not just advice
roadmap.desc: 30-day quick wins, 90-day skill building, and a 12-month career trajectory. Every item has a time estimate, a resource, and an expected impact — so you always know your next step.

howItWorks.sectionSubtitle: From CV upload to full career strategy in about 2 minutes.

step1.title: Upload LinkedIn, CV & Job Posting
step1.desc: Upload your LinkedIn profile PDF and CV, then tell us your target role, experience level, and country. Optionally paste a job description for ATS scoring and a personalized cover letter, or add your GitHub URL for a portfolio analysis.

step2.title: AI Analyzes Everything
step2.desc: Our AI extracts your skills, maps your strengths and gaps, benchmarks salaries, builds a LinkedIn profile plan, generates your career roadmap, and optionally scores your ATS fit — typically in about 2 minutes.

step3.title: Get Your Career Playbook
step3.desc: Explore your results across 11 sections: fit score, job match, LinkedIn profile plan, ATS analysis, cover letter, GitHub portfolio review, strengths & gaps, action plan, recommended roles, and salary benchmarks.

salary.title: Salary Analysis
salary.subtitle: All figures are gross annual (before tax)
salary.sources: Sources: Glassdoor, Levels.fyi, LinkedIn Salary, Stack Overflow Survey (2024-2025)

source.footer: Sources: US Bureau of Labor Statistics OEWS (2024), UK ONS ASHE (2024), Eurostat Structure of Earnings Survey (2022), Stack Overflow Developer Survey (2024), Glassdoor, Levels.fyi
source.tooltip: Salary data is sourced from official government statistics where available (US BLS, UK ONS, Eurostat SES), supplemented by the Stack Overflow Developer Survey (65,000+ respondents) and market research. Data marked 'AI estimate' is generated when no official data is available for this specific role/region combination.

sourceExplain.bls: U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics — Occupational Employment and Wage Statistics (OEWS), 2024 data covering 800+ occupations
sourceExplain.ons: UK Office for National Statistics — Annual Survey of Hours and Earnings (ASHE), 2024 data
sourceExplain.eurostat: EU Eurostat — Structure of Earnings Survey, covering 27+ EU countries
sourceExplain.stackoverflow: Stack Overflow Developer Survey — 65,000+ developer respondents, 2024 edition
sourceExplain.estimate: AI-generated estimate when no official data is available for this role/region. Wider range indicates lower confidence.

linkedin.title: LinkedIn Plan
linkedin.subtitle: Personalized optimization strategy for your LinkedIn profile
linkedin.headline: Headline Suggestions
linkedin.headlineAdvice: Your headline is a search algorithm signal — it's NOT for people already on your profile. Lead with your target role title, front-load keywords recruiters filter for. Max 220 characters.
linkedin.aboutAdvice: First 3 lines show without clicking "see more" — make them count. Write in first person, be specific, end with a call to action.
linkedin.featuredAdvice: Pin 3-6 visual items that prove your headline claims. Use custom thumbnails for maximum impact.
linkedin.skillsNote: Don't remove skills entirely — move them down. LinkedIn allows 50 skills, use all of them.
linkedin.contentAdvice: Consistency beats virality. The hook = first 3 lines before 'see more' — open with a bold claim, a surprising number, or a counterintuitive statement that earns the click.
linkedin.contentFrequency: Recommended: 2-3 posts per week. Rotate formats: story → insight → opinion → short video. Best times: Tue-Thu, 8-10 AM in your target market.
linkedin.connectionsAdvice: Connect with people in your target domain. Personalize every request (3-4 sentences). Commenting on 10 posts/day builds more reach than posting alone.
linkedin.commentingAdvice: Comments > Posts for raw reach. A thoughtful comment on a 100K-follower post surfaces your name to their entire audience — for free. Aim for 10 quality comments per day.
linkedin.commentingPhilosophy: The algorithm rewards engagement. Commenting is the highest-ROI activity on LinkedIn: zero effort to go viral, direct access to others' audiences, and relationship-building that posting alone can't replicate.
linkedin.commentGroup1Reason: Relationship-building + staying visible to your immediate network
linkedin.commentGroup2Reason: Get on their radar before they post a job — engage with their content early
linkedin.commentGroup3Reason: Algorithmic amplification — 3-5 thoughtful comments/week puts you in front of their audience
linkedin.commentGroup4Reason: Community-building — higher response rates, faster relationship formation
linkedin.profileSettingsAdvice: These settings directly affect how recruiters find and contact you. Run through this list before your next outreach sprint.
linkedin.profileLinkAdvice: Your LinkedIn URL, website field, and headline together form your 3-second impression. Make all three reinforce the same message: who you are, what you do, who you do it for.
linkedin.bannerAdvice: Your banner is the first thing visitors see — 1584×396px. Use it to reinforce your headline and core offer. A strong banner with your name, role, and a short value proposition increases profile views by up to 40%.
linkedin.bannerComingSoon: In-app banner generation is coming soon. For now, use Canva (free) with a LinkedIn Banner template. Include: your target role title, 2-3 core skills/keywords, and a brief value statement (e.g. "Helping fintech teams build scalable backend systems").
linkedin.gradeGood: Good foundation — these optimizations will help you stand out
linkedin.gradeNeedsWork: Your profile needs significant updates for your target role

motivation.fitScore: This is an AI estimate of your current readiness — not a definitive verdict. Closing skill gaps is the biggest lever, but a well-optimized, up-to-date CV and LinkedIn profile that clearly signals your target role can push this score higher on their own, without acquiring a single new skill.
motivation.strengths: These are your superpowers. The skills and experiences that make you stand out from other candidates. Lead with these in interviews.
motivation.gaps: Gaps aren't weaknesses — they're your growth roadmap. Every successful career transition involves closing a few of these. You've got this.
motivation.actionPlan: Here's your step-by-step playbook. Start with the 30-day wins to build momentum, then tackle the bigger moves. Small consistent actions compound into career transformation.
motivation.roles: These roles match your unique combination of skills and experience. Some might surprise you — that's often where the best opportunities hide.
motivation.salary: Knowledge is negotiating power. Understanding your market value helps you make confident decisions about your next move.
motivation.jobMatch: Here's how your profile stacks up against this specific opportunity. Use the suggestions below to tailor your application.
motivation.linkedin: Your LinkedIn profile is often the first impression. These optimizations can significantly increase your visibility to recruiters.
motivation.cvGenerator: Your CV is your first impression. This AI-generated framework uses your analysis to create ATS-friendly content optimized for your target role. Copy what works, adapt the rest.
motivation.cvSuggestions: These are targeted changes to align your CV with the specific job posting you provided. Each suggestion shows exactly what to change and why it matters for this role.
motivation.atsScore: Let's make sure your CV gets past the robots. Here's how ATS systems will read your application and what you can optimize.
motivation.cvOptimizer: Your CV analyzed against the job posting — ATS compatibility score, keyword gaps, and specific suggestions to improve your chances.
motivation.coverLetter: A tailored cover letter that highlights your strengths and honestly addresses gaps. Edit it to add your personal touch before sending.
motivation.githubAnalysis: Your GitHub profile tells a story. Here's what hiring managers would see — and how to make it stronger for your target role.
motivation.interviewPrep: You've analyzed the opportunity — now prepare to win the interview. This kit is tailored to the specific job posting, your strengths, and your skill gaps.

cvSuggestions.title: CV Change Suggestions

cvSuggestions.empty: No CV suggestions available. Submit a job posting to get personalized CV change recommendations.

ats.sectionIntro: Let's make sure your CV gets past the robots. Here's how ATS systems will read your application.
ats.scoreGreat: Your CV is well-optimized for this role!
ats.scoreModerate: Good foundation, but there's room to improve your match rate.
ats.scoreNeedsWork: Your CV needs significant optimization for this role.
ats.semanticMatchesDesc: These keywords were found as related terms — consider adding the exact phrasing.

format.noTextDesc: Your PDF appears to be a scanned image — ATS systems cannot read it.
format.noTextFix: Recreate your CV in a text-based format (Word or Google Docs, then export to PDF).
format.tooLongDesc: Your CV is longer than 3 pages — most ATS parsers truncate after page 2-3.
format.tooLongFix: Condense to 2 pages by removing older or less relevant experience.
format.wayTooLongDesc: Your CV is over 5 pages — ATS systems may reject or truncate it.
format.wayTooLongFix: Drastically reduce to 2 pages. Focus on the last 10-15 years of experience.
format.largeFileDesc: Your PDF is larger than 2MB — some upload forms have size limits.
format.largeFileFix: Compress images or remove graphics to reduce file size.
format.multiColumnDesc: Multi-column layout detected — ATS parsers often read columns incorrectly.
format.multiColumnFix: Switch to a single-column layout for maximum ATS compatibility.
format.nonStandardHeadersDesc: Non-standard section headers detected (e.g., 'My Journey' instead of 'Experience').
format.nonStandardHeadersFix: Use standard headers: Experience, Education, Skills, Certifications, Summary.
format.noExperienceDesc: No 'Experience' or 'Work Experience' section header found.
format.noExperienceFix: Add a clearly labeled 'Experience' or 'Professional Experience' section.
format.noSkillsDesc: No 'Skills' section header found — ATS systems index this section for keyword matching.
format.noSkillsFix: Add a 'Skills' or 'Technical Skills' section with relevant keywords.
format.noEducationFix: Add an 'Education' section, even if brief.
format.noEmailDesc: No email address detected in your CV — this is essential for contact.
format.noEmailFix: Add your email address near the top of your CV.
format.specialCharsDesc: High number of special characters detected — may cause parsing errors.
format.specialCharsFix: Replace special characters with standard ASCII equivalents where possible.
format.tooShortDesc: Your CV appears very short — may not contain enough detail for ATS scoring.
format.tooShortFix: Expand your experience and skills sections with more detail.
format.hasImagesDesc: Images or graphics detected — ATS systems ignore visual elements.
format.hasImagesFix: Ensure all important info is in text, not embedded in images or graphics.
format.allGoodDesc: Your CV format looks ATS-friendly! No structural issues detected.

cvOptimizer.sectionIntro: Your CV analyzed against the job posting — ATS compatibility score, keyword gaps, and specific suggestions to improve your chances.

whatIsAts.title: What is an ATS?
whatIsAts.body: An Applicant Tracking System (ATS) is software that companies use to filter resumes before a human ever sees them. Over 90% of large companies use an ATS like Workday, Greenhouse, or Lever. Your resume is scanned for keywords, formatting, and structure — if it doesn't pass, it's automatically rejected.

score.title: ATS Compatibility Score
score.excellent: Excellent — your CV should pass most ATS filters
score.poor: Poor — high risk of automatic rejection

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.initialA: Your very first career analysis is always free and doesn't count toward your weekly limit. Use it to explore the platform and optimize your LinkedIn profile and CV.
faq.resetQ: When do my weekly limits reset?
faq.resetA: All usage counters reset every Monday at midnight UTC. Unused quota does not carry over.
faq.cancelA: Yes. Cancel your subscription at any time from the billing portal. You'll keep Pro access until the end of your current billing period.
faq.dataA: Yes. Your CV and personal data are stored securely with row-level security. We never share your data with third parties. You can delete your data at any time.
